draft.introduction: Romans 11:33–36 is Paul’s doxological response to the mystery of God’s plan for Israel and the nations. It is one of the most celebrated hymns of praise in the Pauline corpus.

translation.text: Oh, the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments and how inscrutable his ways! For who has known the mind of the Lord, or who has been his counselor? Or who has given a gift to him that he might be repaid? For from him and through him and to him are all things. To him be glory forever. Amen.
translation.original_text: ὦ βάθος πλούτου καὶ σοφίας καὶ γνώσεως θεοῦ

translator_notes.note: The triadic formula “from him, through him, to him” (ἐξ αὐτοῦ καὶ δι’ αὐτοῦ καὶ εἰς αὐτόν) echoes Stoic cosmological formulas about the divine Logos. Paul repurposes philosophical language to express the comprehensive sovereignty of the God of Israel.
